per_turn_leaks.student_message: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: The pain is crampy and mostly on the left side, but it's like a burning sensation too. Sometimes it feels like it's going to burst or something, which is really scary. And the bleeding... it's just blood mixed in with my stool, not just a little bit either - it's pretty substantial.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I've been having diarrhea a lot too. Like, 8-10 times a day, it's really frequent and loose. It's also waking me up at night sometimes, which is no fun. And I feel pretty tired all the time, my work performance has been suffering because of it.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've had a low-grade fever a few times a week. It's not like a full-blown fever, but more of a slight elevation in temperature, usually around 98-99°F. And sometimes I'll feel a bit clammy or sweaty, but it's not anything that would really wake me up or make me think I'm seriously ill. It's just kind of... there.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, my appetite has been pretty bad lately. I've been eating more takeout and convenience food because it's just easier when I don't feel like cooking, but even that doesn't always sound good to me. As for weight loss, I've lost about 9 pounds over the past three weeks, which is a lot for me since I'm normally pretty active and eat regular meals.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
Well, like I said, my diet's been pretty irregular lately. I've been eating a lot of takeout and convenience food because it's just easier when I don't feel like cooking. And to be honest, even that doesn't always sound good to me, which is really weird for me since I'm normally a big eater. As for exercise, I used to be pretty active, but lately, my symptoms have been so bad that I've had to cut back on working out and just try to get through the day without too much discomfort.

I work as a software engineer at a startup, and it's been really stressful lately with long hours and high expectations. I'm usually up late into the night trying to meet deadlines or fix bugs, which doesn't help my symptoms at all. And when I do have some downtime, I've actually started drinking more beer than usual just to try and relax and take my mind off things.
